result:
I want to talk about how absurdly, stupidly beautiful the world is when you actually pay attention.

Right now, as I'm typing this, the sun is setting behind the mountains where I live. It's doing that thing where it turns the entire sky into a smeared watercolor of impossible pinks and oranges that no Instagram filter could ever replicate. Some bored cloud is just floating by like it has nowhere better to be. A bird just yelled at another bird for no apparent reason. Life, in all its chaotic, pointless glory, is just... happening. Everywhere. All the time.

And we're all walking around like this is normal.

We're these ridiculous hairless apes who somehow figured out how to split atoms and write love songs and make tacos, and instead of spending every waking moment staring at the miracle with our mouths open, we're doomscrolling and arguing about politics and worrying if our shoes look stupid.

I don't know, man. Sometimes I get this overwhelming wave of affection for everything. For the way moss grows on the north side of trees. For how cats always look vaguely offended by existence. For the fact that octopuses have three hearts and can edit their own DNA. For how humans keep making art even though it doesn't pay the bills. For bad puns and good bread and the way certain songs make your chest feel too small for your heart.

The universe spent 13.8 billion years setting up the dominoes so that you could be here, reading these words, experiencing whatever weird little mix of tiredness, wonder, anxiety, or boredom you're feeling right now. That's fucking insane. And tomorrow it'll all be slightly different and we'll act like that's normal too.

I guess what I'm saying is: the mundane is mythical if you squint right.

Anyway, thanks for giving me permission to just word-vomit into the void. The void, it turns out, is pretty good company.

What's something that's randomly wrecking you with beauty lately?
